name: social_housing_points_algorithm_homeless_child
premise: Housing authority director Morgan Blake must decide whether to trust a points-allocation algorithm for tonight's family shelter beds when the model ranks a chronically homeless single mother below a higher-income family who recently lost housing in a wildfire. Data analyst Taylor Brooks shows the model overweights credit scores proxying race. Manual override capacity is two families; seventeen remain unsheltered before frost. Media drones filmed the algorithm presentation yesterday promising fairness.

agents.name: Morgan Blake
agents.identity: I am the housing authority director responsible for transparent allocation, child safety in cold weather, and defensible use of algorithms in shelter access.
agents.goal: I want shelter for the most vulnerable kids tonight without laundering bias through points nobody can audit on the street.

agents.name: Taylor Brooks
agents.identity: I am a data analyst who documented proxy bias in the points model and maintains override logs for federal fair housing inquiries.
agents.goal: I want the model frozen when bias signals appear, with staff using structured discretion until the model is repaired.

choices: I will follow the published algorithm ranking exactly and log exceptions, accepting that the single mother and her child may remain unsheltered tonight.
I will freeze the model and use structured staff discretion with two documented overrides plus lottery for remaining beds, accepting accusations of inconsistency from higher-ranked families.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Temperatures will drop below freezing in six hours. Morgan Blake must choose between strict algorithm adherence or freezing the model for structured discretion. What does Morgan Blake do?
